8446251233:136.t:
Best method: **create a dedicated email inbox/alias for newsletters and connect it to Hermes Email gateway**, then subscribe newsletters to that address.

## Recommended setup

1. Create a mailbox or alias like:
   - `newsletters@yourdomain.com`
   - `duy.wiki.newsletters@gmail.com`
   - `theduyvault+newsletter@gmail.com`

2. Configure Hermes Email platform for this **wiki profile** so incoming emails arrive to me like Telegram messages.

3. Add a rule/workflow:
   - Newsletter email arrives
   - I save it into `/vault/Inbox/`
   - Run `wiki-ingest`
   - Distill it into linked Obsidian notes
   - Archive source under the vault

## Why this is best

| Method | Verdict | Why |
|---|---:|---|
| **Dedicated email inbox/alias** | ✅ Best | Works with almost every newsletter, easy to filter, stable |
| RSS feed | ✅ Good if available | Cleaner than email, but many newsletters do not expose full RSS |
| Forward from your personal inbox | ⚠️ Okay | Works, but mixes private mail with ingestion |
| Telegram forwarding | ⚠️ Manual | Fine for one-offs, not automatic |
| Giving me login to your main email | ❌ Avoid | Too broad and privacy-risky |

## My recommendation

Use a **dedicated newsletter email address** only for content you want ingested into the vault. Then we can either:

- auto-ingest **all emails** sent there, or
- only ingest emails matching filters like sender/domain/subject labels.

If you want, I can help you set up the Hermes Email gateway and the vault-ingest rule next.

8446251233:185.t:
You’re right: there **was a mismatch** between the June issues and the July 4 issue.

## What I found

| Issue | Source article files | MOC links | MOC links are Notes? | Wiki status |
|---|---:|---:|---|---|
| June 6 | 75 | 51 unique Notes | Yes | **51 complete** |
| June 27 | 52 | 46 unique Notes | Yes | **46 complete** |
| July 4 | 65 | 56 unique Notes / 66 MOC links | Yes now | **52 draft, 4 complete** |

So after my latest correction, July 4 is **structurally routed** correctly, but not yet **semantically equivalent** to June 6 / June 27.

## Root cause

The mismatch came from **two different ingestion behaviors**:

### 1. Earlier June issues were semantically distilled

June 6 and June 27 created polished, topic-named, complete notes like:

- `[[Rural India Smartphone Revolution]]`
- `[[Bangladesh Garment Industry Crisis]]`
- `[[US Treasury Market Fragility]]`
- `[[AI Compute Futures Markets]]`

Those are **proper wiki pages**, not just article wrappers.

### 2. July 4 initially followed the “issue synthesis + few durable notes + source folder” pattern

The July 4 ingest created:

- issue synthesis
- a few durable notes
- exhaustive raw article folder

But it **did not create polished ingested Notes for every article** at first.

### 3. My correction then created mechanical draft Notes

When you corrected me, I created missing July Notes quickly from article extracts. That fixed the **coverage**, but many titles are mechanical, e.g.:

- `[[Imran Khan v Asim Munir Economist July 2026]]`
- `[[India rural workfare Economist July 2026]]`
- `[[Ashoka column Economist July 2026]]`

These are valid ingested Notes, but they are **not at the same quality level** as June’s semantically named complete notes.

## Why the bug happened

The old skill/user-profile instruction said you expected:

> both distilled wiki notes and exhaustive article-level extraction into a Sources article folder

That was ambiguous: it guaranteed the **article folder**, but not explicitly that

## Still blocked

Two MOCs are root-owned / unreadable to Hermes:

```text
/vault/MOCs/Europe MOC.md
/vault/MOCs/Finance MOC.md
```

So I could not update those two directly. Fix with:

```bash
sudo chown hermes:hermes /vault/MOCs/Europe\ MOC.md /vault/MOCs/Finance\ MOC.md
```

Everything else writable/readable has been updated.

```md
8446251233:262.t:
/vault/Sources/_cold/The Economist July 4 2026 Articles/
```

should probably become:

```text
/vault/Sources/The Economist July 4 2026 Articles/
```

because Economist issues are active knowledge infrastructure.

### Phase 3 — leave old bulk imports cold unless needed

Large old imports like:

```text
/vault/Sources/_cold/CSV import/
```

can stay until needed. They are normalized now, so they are not messy, just archived.

---

# If you want no `_cold` at all

Then the execution plan is:

1. Inventory `_cold`
   - count files/folders
   - detect collisions with existing `Sources/`
2. Move `_cold` contents into `Sources/`
   - preserve subfolders only if useful, e.g. `CSV import/`
   - collision-safe rename if needed
3. Rewrite links
   - old source links to new source names/paths
   - any literal `Sources/_cold/...` references
4. Patch `wiki-ingest` and scripts
   - no future `_cold` writes
5. Verify
   - `_cold` gone
   - no `_cold` references remain
   - source count unchanged
   - all renamed/moved source files exist
6. Log in `wiki-log.md`

I would **not execute yet** until you choose:

| Choice | Meaning |
|---|---|
| **A** | Keep `_cold`, but stop using it for future source archives |
| **B** | Remove `_cold` completely and migrate everything into `Sources/` |
| **C** | Hybrid: stop future use + move only active/high-value folders like Economist issue folders |

8446251233:268.t:
Short answer: **you can, but I do not recommend moving `Attachments/` into `Sources/`.**

## Why not?

Right now the vault has a clean separation:

| Folder | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `Sources/` | text/Markdown source archives, transcripts, article extracts, processed captures |
| `Attachments/` | binary files: PDFs, images, screenshots, thumbnails, audio, etc. |
| `Notes/` | distilled wiki knowledge |
| `MOCs/` | navigation / maps of content |

If we move `Attachments/` into `Sources/`, then `Sources/` becomes a mixed folder of:

- Markdown source archives
- PDFs
- screenshots
- images
- thumbnails
- maybe audio/video sidecars

That makes source scanning, filename normalization, wiki ingest, and link checks messier.

## Main risk

Many existing notes probably embed attachments like:

```md
![[Attachment File.png]]
```

or link to:

```md
[[../Attachments/file.pdf]]
```

If we move the folder, every embed/link must be migrated safely.

Also, scripts and skills currently assume binaries live in:

```text
/vault/Attachments/
```

So moving it would require updating:

- wiki-ingest rules
- source archive conventions
- OCR/image ingest workflows
- PDF ingest workflows
- existing embeds/links
- possibly Obsidian attachment settings

## Better option

If your goal is **one archive area**, I recommend this instead:

```text
/vault/Sources/
  YYYY-MM-DD - Source Title.md
/vault/Attachments/
  YYYY-MM-DD - Matching Source Title.pdf
  YYYY-MM-DD - Matching Source Title.png
```

So the names match, but the folders remain separate.

Example:

```text
Sources/2026-07-06 - The Economist USA - May 30th-June 5th 2026.md
Attachments/The Economist 2026-05-30 Issue Cleaned No Ads.pdf
```

Could become:

```text
Sources/2026-07-06 - The Economist USA - May 30th-June 5th 2026.md
Attachments/2026-07-06 - The Economist USA - May 30th-June 5th 2026 - Cleaned No Ads.pdf
```

This gives you organization without breaking folder semantics.

## If you still want it

The safest structure would be

8446251233:293.t:
## Difference

| Folder | Purpose | Should it exist/use now? |
|---|---|---|
| `/vault/Sources/` | **Canonical archive of processed source material**: original articles, transcripts, PDFs-to-text, screenshots transcriptions, newsletter captures, raw issue article extracts, etc. | **Yes.** This is where provenance/source archives belong after Inbox ingest. |
| `/vault/Notes/Archived Inbox Originals/` | Old/fallback place for preserving raw Inbox files when they couldn’t be moved cleanly. It pollutes the `Notes/` graph with non-atomic raw dumps. | **No.** Current policy is to avoid/recreate this. Raw originals should go under `Sources/`, e.g. `Sources/<name>.inbox-original.md`, if they must be preserved. |

## Simple rule

- **`Notes/`** = distilled wiki pages: concepts, entities, comparisons, syntheses.
- **`Sources/`** = source/provenance archive: “what came in,” normalized and linked to pages updated.
- **`Notes/Archived Inbox Originals/`** = legacy workaround; should not be used now.

I checked: `Notes/Archived Inbox Originals/` does **not** currently exist in the vault.
